executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict remains at MAJOR severity with 858 incidents in 24h. The assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei has triggered a new phase — Iran officially confirms his death and Hezbollah has formally entered the war, targeting Haifa. US forces suffered first aircraft loss (F-15 in Kuwait) while Iranian strikes hit Bahrain (US 5th Fleet), UAE (AWS data centre), and Dubai civilian targets. UK, France, Germany join US coalition against Iran. No de-escalation pathway visible; Trump suggests war may last 4 weeks.
whats_new: Hezbollah formally announces entry into war, launches rockets and drones at Haifa in retaliation for Khamenei's assassination
Pentagon confirms no intelligence indicated Iran planned to attack US forces first before the killing of Supreme Leader
WHO Director-General expresses concern over targeting of Gandhi Hospital in Tehran — civilian medical infrastructure now hit
British authorities evacuate non-essential staff from RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus after drone strike
IDF Chief of Staff announces Israel has launched offensive campaign against Hezbollah — no longer defensive operations only
confirmed_events: Iran confirms death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei (multiple Iranian sources, official condolences)
American F-15 shot down in Kuwaiti airspace — first US aircraft loss in current conflict, pilot survived
Iranian missiles hit Bahrain (US 5th Fleet base at Juffair) with 9-10 impacts, confirmed by FIRMS satellite data — 1 civilian killed, 2 injured from debris
Iranian strike on AWS data centre in UAE causing fire and power outage — direct attack on US-aligned infrastructure
UK, France, Germany announce coordination with US against Iran — European coalition expansion confirmed
unverified_emerging: Pentagon brief: Trump 'Operation Epic Rage' claims to have eliminated entire Iranian military leadership and destroyed hundreds of targets — unverified scale of claims
Unconfirmed reports of violent explosions in Dubai and Doha — if verified would mark major escalation into Gulf cities (single-source from Al Jazeera/Al Arabiya)
Reports of Khamenei family members targeted in strikes — unverified
Growing internal Pentagon concern conflict could spiral out of control — Washington Post citing anonymous officials

people_travel: Emirates Airlines suspended all services to/from Dubai — major hub closure
Hundreds of flights cancelled, worst travel chaos since Covid pandemic
Key Middle Eastern air hubs remain closed
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz disrupted by conflict — oil shipping affected
Platts fuel price assessments disrupted due to conflict
Port operations in Dubai/UAE impacted by strikes
market_macro: Asian stocks fall on conflict escalation

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict enters third day with no de-escalation signals. Iran confirmed death of Supreme Leader → expect heightened retaliatory operations from Tehran and proxies. Hezbollah formally at war with Israel opens new northern front. US-Iran direct confrontation expands geographically (Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE, Cyprus). Qatar/Gulf states increasingly targeted. European coalition now aligned with US. Military operations likely to continue at current intensity for minimum 4 weeks based on Trump statements.
escalation_risks: Iranian strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure (Saudi, Qatar, Oman) — Strait of Hormuz could be fully blocked
Regional war expansion: Iraq may be drawn in as US strike platform; Iranian proxies in Syria/Iraq may activate
Nuclear facility targeting — Bushehr or other nuclear sites not yet struck but at risk
NATO Article 5 trigger — Cyprus base attack on UK (NATO member) increases escalation risk
Oil supply shock — if Strait of Hormuz closes or Saudi facilities hit, global energy crisis
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No diplomatic pathway currently visible. Ali Larijani (Supreme National Security Council) rejected negotiations, calling US 'immersed in delusions.' Iran has not named successor to Khamenei, creating succession uncertainty. US demands unconditional surrender of weapons from Iranian military — Tehran unlikely to accept. European allies caught off guard by US decision may pressure for negotiated settlement but have limited leverage. Internal Iranian instability (IRGC warning against 'disruption') could create pressure but unlikely to shift regime posture in near term.
